text.title: API Versioning Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Single handler dispatches between v1 and v2 response shapes via if/else inside business logic.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated v1/v2 as 'duplication to remove'.
antipattern.fix: Move v1 to a frozen read-only module; v2 is a separate module/router. Forbid shared handlers in code review.

antipattern.symptom: 2-person team adopts Stripe-style date-based versioning with per-customer pinning.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents read the Stripe blog and copy the pattern without weighing operational cost.
antipattern.fix: Use URL path versioning at small scale; date-based requires a transformation pipeline that small teams cannot maintain.

antipattern.symptom: Agent decides 'v1 is deprecated' and deletes the handler in the same PR as the 410 Gone flip.
antipattern.root-cause: Sunset workflow not encoded as two PRs.
antipattern.fix: Block any PR that deletes a v1 handler unless preceded by 30 days of zero traffic AND a prior 410 Gone PR.

antipattern.symptom: Deprecation is announced in a blog post but the route still returns 200 with no Sunset/Deprecation headers.
antipattern.fix: Add a global middleware that injects Deprecation/Sunset/Link headers for any route marked deprecated in ops/sunset.yaml.
